worth_it_explanation: DV360 applies Google's own invalid traffic (IVT) filtering by default, but that baseline does not substitute for an active investigation of suspicious placements or the application of fraud-specific pre-bid segments from a verification vendor. These checks are fast to run inside the DV360 interface and the verification vendor's dashboard. The work is pattern recognition, not a rebuild, and the fixes are targeting exclusions and list additions that take effect from the next impression served.
intro: Display and Video 360 (DV360) filters general invalid traffic (GIVT), meaning bots and non-human activity identified through standard means such as data center signatures and known crawler lists, before billing. Sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT), which mimics real users and requires behavioral analysis to detect, is harder to filter at the platform level and benefits from additional controls. These checks confirm that DV360's native IVT protections are in place, that suspicious domains and apps showing abnormal performance patterns are identified and excluded, and that fraud-specific pre-bid segments or exclusion lists from a verification vendor are applied to the highest-risk inventory.

audit_checks.title: DV360 invalid traffic filtering has not been confirmed active at the line item level
audit_checks.impact_rationale: DV360 applies Google's own IVT filtering by default, but a line item targeting open exchange inventory with no additional fraud controls relies entirely on that baseline. If the baseline is overridden or a line item is configured to buy from inventory sources that bypass standard filtering, bot traffic reaches delivery and inflates impression counts without producing any real audience signal.

educate.base:
Display and Video 360 (DV360) applies Google's invalid traffic (IVT) filtering to all inventory it buys. The filtering catches general invalid traffic (GIVT), such as data center traffic, known bots, and web crawlers, before the impression is counted as delivered. Google credits back IVT it detects after delivery through its invalid activity detection process.

GIVT filtering is on by default and cannot be turned off at the advertiser or line item level. However, the strength of the protection depends on where the line item is buying. Open exchange inventory carries more IVT exposure than private marketplace (PMP) deals with known publishers. The open exchange is a broader and less-vetted supply pool. A line item targeting only PMP deals from established publishers inherits the publisher's own traffic quality controls in addition to DV360's baseline.

Sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT) is a separate category. SIVT mimics real user behavior through mechanisms such as ad stacking, pixel stuffing, and bot networks that cycle through real browser environments. Google's baseline filtering addresses SIVT it can identify, but detection is harder than for GIVT and the coverage is not complete. A third-party verification vendor's pre-bid fraud segment narrows the pool further by applying the vendor's own SIVT scoring before DV360 places the bid.

A few signs point to IVT protections being inadequate:
  • Line items buying exclusively from open exchange with no additional fraud controls beyond the DV360 baseline
  • No pre-bid fraud segment from a verification vendor attached to high-volume open exchange line items
  • DV360's 'Active View' and impression reports show delivery numbers that are inconsistent with expected reach for the targeting, suggesting non-human activity may be inflating counts
You may want to review the inventory source settings for each active line item alongside the verification vendor's fraud exposure report before deciding which controls to add.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser, impressions served to bots contribute nothing to the funnel but consume budget. You may want to prioritize applying a pre-bid fraud segment to any open exchange line items driving traffic to product pages. Those are the line items where IVT inflates click and session metrics in downstream analytics.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, IVT on display or video line items inflates delivery counts without producing form submissions or conversions. You may want to confirm whether the verification vendor's fraud exposure report shows elevated IVT rates on line items that also show low conversion rates. That combination points to bot traffic consuming budget that should reach real prospective leads.

learn_more.source_title: Invalid traffic and ad fraud in Display and Video 360
Trustworthy Accountability Group: Certified Against Fraud

investigate.intro: Confirm that DV360's baseline IVT filtering is in place and identify any open exchange line items that carry no additional fraud controls.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: In DV360, open the advertiser and navigate to 'Line items'. Filter for active line items.
For each line item, open the targeting settings and identify the inventory source. Note whether the line item buys from the open exchange, from PMP deals, or both.

For open exchange line items, check the brand safety section and the audience targeting section for any pre-bid fraud segments from a connected verification vendor such as DoubleVerify (DV) or Integral Ad Science (IAS). If no fraud segment is listed as a targeting exclusion, record the line item name.

Open the reporting section and pull an impression report by domain or app for the open exchange line items flagged in the previous step. Note any domains or apps showing an unusually high share of impressions relative to their expected reach.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, for each active open exchange line item, whether a pre-bid fraud segment from a verification vendor is attached as a targeting exclusion. A line item with a fraud segment applied, or one that buys exclusively from PMP deals with vetted publishers, passes. A line item buying from the open exchange with no fraud segment and no other fraud control beyond the DV360 baseline is a fail recorded with the line item name and the inventory source setting.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the inventory source settings and any pre-bid fraud segment status for each active open exchange line item above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Display and Video 360 (DV360) has open exchange line items relying on Google's baseline invalid traffic (IVT) filtering alone, with no additional fraud controls applied. The baseline catches general invalid traffic such as known bots and data center traffic, but sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT) that mimics real users requires deeper detection than the platform's default layer provides. {{voice_recommend}} connecting a third-party verification vendor and applying a pre-bid fraud exclusion segment to the open exchange line items, then reviewing the vendor's fraud exposure report for any domains or apps showing elevated IVT rates. {{voice_recommend}} shifting budget toward private marketplace (PMP) deals with vetted publishers for line items where fraud exposure is highest.

audit_checks.title: Suspicious domains and apps with abnormal performance patterns are not excluded
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A domain or app that delivers a high volume of impressions with a click-through rate far above or below what real users produce is exhibiting a performance pattern that may indicate non-human activity. Leaving those placements in the active pool means budget continues reaching them on every subsequent flight without any investigation of whether the traffic is real.

educate.base:
IVT filtering removes impressions that match known fraud signatures, but it does not remove domains or apps that exhibit suspicious behavior patterns without triggering a known filter. A placement can pass Google's IVT detection and still deliver results that real user behavior would not produce: click-through rates an order of magnitude higher than the account average, interaction rates that spike on a narrow set of apps with no corresponding conversion activity, or reach numbers inconsistent with the declared audience size.

Monitoring placement-level performance in DV360 is the standard way to identify these patterns. The placement report in DV360 breaks down delivery by domain and app, and sorting by impression share, click-through rate (CTR), or interaction rate surfaces outliers. A domain contributing 15% of impressions with a CTR three times the account average and zero conversions is a reasonable candidate for investigation and exclusion.

The exclusion itself is applied as a URL exclusion in the advertiser-level or line-item-level brand safety settings. Once a domain or app bundle is on the exclusion list, DV360 will not buy inventory from it on future flights.

A few signs point to suspicious placements remaining in the active pool:
  • The placement report shows one or more domains or apps with a CTR more than twice the account average and no accompanying conversion activity
  • A small number of app bundle IDs account for a disproportionate share of impressions in connected TV (CTV) or in-app line items
  • The URL exclusion list has not been updated since the campaign launched, suggesting no placement review has taken place
You may want to pull the placement report for the trailing 30 days and sort by impression share before building the exclusion list, rather than relying on intuition about which placements to block.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser, unusually high CTR placements that produce no add-to-cart or purchase events are a signal worth investigating. You may want to cross-reference the placement report with the site analytics tool. Genuine user clicks from real placements leave a session trace that bot traffic from IVT-generating domains does not.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, a placement delivering a high share of impressions with a high CTR but a form submission rate at zero is a reliable flag. You may want to confirm whether those clicks appear in the site analytics tool as sessions at all. Click counts in DV360 that have no matching session data in the analytics tool point to click inflation rather than real user behavior.

learn_more.source_title: Exclude specific URLs from your campaign

investigate.intro: Pull a placement report for the last 30 days and identify domains or apps with performance patterns inconsistent with real user behavior.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: In DV360, navigate to 'Reports' and create or open a standard report. Set the date range to the last 30 days.
Add 'Domain or app' as a dimension. Add impressions, clicks, click-through rate (CTR), and conversions as metrics.
Sort by impression share descending to find the highest-volume placements. Then sort by CTR descending to find placements with an unusually high CTR relative to the account average.
Flag any domain or app where CTR is more than twice the account average and conversions are at zero. Note the impression share each flagged placement represents.

In the advertiser settings, navigate to 'Brand safety' and then 'URL exclusions'. Add each flagged domain or app as an exclusion after confirming it is not a placement the account intentionally targets through a PMP deal.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner pulls the placement report for the last 30 days and records the domains or apps with a CTR more than twice the account average and zero conversions, along with their impression share. Placements that have been reviewed and are excluded or intentionally retained with documented rationale pass. Placements that have never been reviewed, or that meet the anomalous-performance threshold and remain in the active pool without documentation, are a fail recorded with the placement name and the specific metrics that triggered the flag.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the flagged placements and their CTR and conversion data above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Display and Video 360 (DV360) placement report shows domains or apps with performance patterns inconsistent with real user activity: elevated click-through rates and negligible conversion output relative to the account average. These placements pass platform-level invalid traffic (IVT) filtering but exhibit behavior that warrants exclusion. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the placement report for the last 30 days, adding the flagged domains and apps to the URL exclusion list at the advertiser level, and establishing a regular placement review cadence so new outliers are caught before they accumulate significant spend.

audit_checks.title: Fraud exclusion lists and verification fraud segments are not applied to high-risk inventory
audit_checks.impact_rationale: High-risk inventory types, including open exchange programmatic video, CTV, and in-app mobile, carry higher IVT rates than other formats because the impression volumes involved make them attractive targets for fraud. Without fraud-specific exclusion lists or verification fraud segments applied to these line items, the account has no proactive control beyond the platform's baseline and the placement review process.

educate.base:
Some inventory types carry structurally higher IVT exposure than others. Open exchange programmatic video and connected TV (CTV) are frequent targets because the higher cost-per-mille (CPM) that video and CTV commands makes each fraudulent impression more profitable to generate. In-app mobile inventory, particularly from apps that do not have an established publisher relationship with the exchange, also shows elevated fraud rates in industry measurement.

Two tools address this at the line item level in DV360. The first is a fraud-specific pre-bid segment from a verification vendor such as DoubleVerify (DV) or Integral Ad Science (IAS). These segments represent inventory that the vendor has scored as high-fraud-risk based on behavioral signals and are applied as a targeting exclusion on the line item, so DV360 does not bid on impressions the vendor has already flagged. The second is a curated exclusion list, such as a made-for-advertising (MFA) site list or a known low-quality app bundle list, that the account builds or imports and applies at the advertiser level.

Neither tool eliminates IVT on its own. They reduce the pool of inventory the account buys from by removing supply that has already been identified as problematic. The combination of a fraud pre-bid segment and a maintained exclusion list gives the line item two layers of proactive control before any impression is delivered.

A few signs point to fraud exclusion lists and segments not being applied:
  • Video or CTV line items have no pre-bid fraud segment in their targeting settings
  • In-app mobile line items are targeting all apps rather than a curated list of quality app bundles
  • The advertiser has no exclusion list that covers known low-quality domains or app bundles
You may want to ask the verification vendor whether they provide a pre-built low-quality app bundle exclusion list for DV360 in addition to the pre-bid segment, as many vendors maintain one that is ready to apply.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser running video or CTV, high-IVT impressions inflate the view count without contributing to product consideration. You may want to confirm whether the video and CTV line items have a fraud segment applied, and whether the CPM and delivery data from those line items is consistent with the reach expected from the targeting.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, in-app mobile line items are particularly worth reviewing. In-app environments can generate high impression volumes that look productive on delivery metrics but produce no form submissions. You may want to check whether in-app mobile line items have both a fraud pre-bid segment and an app bundle exclusion list applied before deciding how much budget to allocate to that format.

learn_more.source_title: Brand safety and suitability in Display and Video 360

investigate.intro: Identify the high-risk inventory line items in the account, then confirm whether fraud exclusion lists or pre-bid fraud segments are applied to each.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: In DV360, navigate to 'Line items' and filter for active line items. Sort or filter to isolate video, CTV, and in-app mobile line items, as these format types carry higher IVT exposure.

For each identified line item, open the targeting settings. In the brand safety or audiences section, look for any pre-bid fraud segment from a connected verification vendor such as DoubleVerify (DV) or Integral Ad Science (IAS). Record which line items have a fraud segment and which do not.

Open the advertiser-level brand safety settings and check the URL exclusion list for any entries that represent known low-quality domains or app bundle IDs. Note whether the list exists and when it was last updated.

If a verification vendor is connected, log in to the vendor's platform and check whether a pre-built low-quality app bundle or MFA exclusion list is available for the account. Note whether it has been applied to the relevant line items in DV360.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, for each active video, CTV, and in-app mobile line item, whether a pre-bid fraud segment from a verification vendor is applied and whether the account has an exclusion list covering known low-quality placements. A line item with a fraud segment applied, or one buying exclusively from PMP deals with vetted publishers, passes. A line item in a high-risk format buying from the open exchange with no fraud segment and no exclusion list is a fail recorded with the line item name and the format type.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the fraud control status for each video, CTV, and in-app mobile line item above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Display and Video 360 (DV360) video, connected TV (CTV), and in-app mobile line items are running without fraud-specific pre-bid segments or exclusion lists applied. These formats carry elevated invalid traffic (IVT) exposure because their higher CPMs make fraudulent impressions more profitable to generate. {{voice_recommend}} applying a pre-bid fraud exclusion segment from the connected verification vendor to each high-risk format line item, and maintaining an exclusion list of known low-quality domains and app bundles at the advertiser level. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the verification vendor's fraud exposure report for these line items after the controls are applied to confirm the IVT rate decreases.
